_basis: IEC 60364-6 Edition 2 (2016) with Amendment 1 (2023). Covers initial and periodic verification.
_note: Verification is the process of confirming that an installation complies with IEC 60364. It comprises two stages: Inspection (visual) and Testing (measurement). Both must be completed and documented before the installation is declared safe for use.

initial_verification.description: Carried out on every new installation, and on additions or alterations to an existing installation.
initial_verification.when: Before the installation is put into service — or before the section affected by a modification is energised.
initial_verification.who: A competent person — one with knowledge of IEC 60364, the test equipment used, and the relevant hazards.
initial_verification.output: An Electrical Installation Certificate (or equivalent national document) confirming the installation complies with the applicable standard at the time of inspection.

periodic_verification.description: Re-inspection and re-testing of an installation at regular intervals to detect deterioration or modification.
periodic_verification.purpose: Installations age — insulation degrades, connections loosen, RCDs drift, earthing electrodes corrode. Periodic inspection finds hazards before they cause incidents.

intervals_guidance.domestic: Every 10 years (or on change of occupancy)
intervals_guidance.note: These are recommended intervals. National codes may specify different mandatory intervals.

periodic_verification.output: Periodic Inspection Report (PIR) or Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR) — documents any departures from IEC 60364 and classifies them by risk.

inspection_checks._order: Inspection must be completed BEFORE testing — testing while installation is damaged or non-compliant may miss hazards or damage test equipment.

items_to_inspect.item: Methods of protection against electric shock

checks: No chafing or mechanical damage visible
No bypassed or defeated protection devices
Fuses of correct rating and type
Cable types appropriate for environment (temperature, chemicals, UV)
Cable entries protected against mechanical damage
CPC continuity connections visible and correctly made
Main bonding connections in place for all metallic services
Warning notices present (voltage, phase, test intervals)
Hazard warnings at points of danger
No bare copper visible outside terminals
Correct polarity at socket outlets (visual check)
No damage to insulation at terminations

testing_sequence._critical_order: Tests must be performed in this order. Subsequent tests assume previous tests passed.

test_1_continuity_of_CPC.description: Verify the circuit protective conductor (CPC) forms a continuous, low-resistance path from the furthest point of every circuit back to the MET.
test_1_continuity_of_CPC.method: Wander lead method: use a calibrated continuity tester (low-resistance ohmmeter). Measure resistance between each exposed-conductive-part and the MET.
test_1_continuity_of_CPC.pass_criteria: CPC resistance should be consistent with the calculated value for the conductor cross-section and length. Abnormally high readings indicate broken or inadequate CPC.

typical_values.note: Record values for comparison at future periodic inspections — trend more important than absolute value

test_1_continuity_of_CPC.when_not_required: Not required for consumer units directly supplied at MET where CPC connection is visible and confirmed by inspection.

test_2_continuity_of_main_bonding.description: Verify that all main equipotential bonding conductors are connected and have acceptable resistance.
test_2_continuity_of_main_bonding.method: Measure resistance between each bonded metallic service (water, gas, structural steel) and the MET.
test_2_continuity_of_main_bonding.pass_criteria: Resistance should be consistent with conductor size and route. Typically < 0.05Ω for bonding conductors < 5m long.
test_2_continuity_of_main_bonding.note: Main bonding does not need to be measured on every periodic inspection if it is visually confirmed intact — but must be measured on initial verification.

test_3_insulation_resistance.description: Verify that the insulation between conductors and between live conductors and earth is adequate — no breakdown or tracking paths.
test_3_insulation_resistance.method: Apply a DC test voltage between live conductors (L and N bridged) and earth, with all loads disconnected. Use a calibrated insulation tester.

pass_criteria.below_1_MΩ: Investigate fault — check for moisture ingress, damaged cable, or faulty equipment left connected
pass_criteria.note: Test with all equipment disconnected. RCDs, surge protective devices, and electronic ballasts will give false low readings if left connected.

test_3_insulation_resistance.warning: Never apply insulation test voltage to RCDs, SPDs, electronic ballasts, dimmer switches, or occupancy sensors while connected. Disconnect or bypass before testing.

test_4_polarity.description: Verify that single-pole protective devices are connected in the line (phase) conductor only, and that socket outlets are wired with correct polarity (L to L, N to N, E to E).
test_4_polarity.method: Visual inspection and continuity testing between phase and line terminal of each socket outlet/accessory.
test_4_polarity.pass_criteria: No N-switched or N-fused connections. All accessories correctly polarised.
test_4_polarity.critical_for: MCBs and fuses — if installed in neutral rather than line, they will not protect correctly and may leave live conductors accessible after apparent disconnection.

test_5_earth_fault_loop_impedance_Zs.description: Measure the actual earth fault loop impedance at representative points and verify it does not exceed Zs_max for the protective device protecting each circuit.
test_5_earth_fault_loop_impedance_Zs.method: Use a calibrated loop impedance tester. Measure Zs at the furthest accessible point of each circuit (or representative circuits on large installations).

pass_criteria.TN_systems: Measured Zs ≤ Zs_max for the protective device (from part4-41-electric-shock.json). Apply a multiplier of 0.8 to Zs_max to account for the difference between cold test temperature and hot operating temperature.
pass_criteria.TT_systems: Ra × IΔn ≤ 50V — measure Ra (electrode resistance) separately. Loop impedance measurement in TT will include the earth electrode resistance.
pass_criteria.correction_for_temperature: Measured Zs is at ambient temperature. Operating temperature (hot) resistance is higher. Apply factor: Zs_hot = Zs_measured × 1.2 (approximate for copper PVC at 70°C). Verify Zs_hot ≤ Zs_max.

test_5_earth_fault_loop_impedance_Zs.important: Zs_max values from IEC 60364-4-41 are for HOT conductors (at operating temperature). Measured values are COLD. Must apply temperature correction to be valid.

test_6_earth_electrode_resistance.description: Measure the resistance of the earth electrode to the general mass of earth.
test_6_earth_electrode_resistance.method: Fall-of-potential (three-electrode) method, or clamp-on method for installations with multiple earth points.
test_6_earth_electrode_resistance.when_required: Always for TT systems. For TN-C-S, if a supplementary electrode is installed. For IT systems.
test_6_earth_electrode_resistance.pass_criteria: Ra ≤ 50V / IΔn for the protective device. Absolute maximum 200Ω for effective protection — ideally ≤ 50Ω.

test_7_RCD_operation.description: Verify that every RCD trips within the required time at its rated residual current and does NOT trip at half rated residual current.
test_7_RCD_operation.method: Use a calibrated RCD tester. Apply test currents at IΔn and at 0.5 × IΔn. Measure trip time.
test_7_RCD_operation.test_position: Test at 0°, 180°, and 90° phase angles where the tester allows — to catch RCDs that only detect certain phases of the AC waveform.
test_7_RCD_operation.push_button_test: After electrical test, verify physical push-button operates the mechanical trip mechanism. Document that button was pressed.
test_7_RCD_operation.note: An RCD that passes the timed test may still fail if the push-button has never been operated — the mechanical trip can seize. Regular monthly user testing by pressing the button is important.

test_8_functional_testing.description: Verify that the installation works correctly under normal operating conditions.
checks: All protective devices can be switched OFF and ON

required_information: Details of installation: address, supply, type of earthing system
Designer's declaration that design complies with IEC 60364 (or applicable national code)
Constructor's declaration that erection complies with design and IEC 60364
Inspector's declaration that inspection and testing has been completed and results comply
Schedule of all tests performed with measured values
Schedule of circuits and protective device ratings
Details of any departures from the standard (with justification)

classification_of_findings.C1_danger_present: Risk of injury — immediate remedial action required. Installation should not be used until rectified.
classification_of_findings.C2_potentially_dangerous: Urgent remedial work required. Installation should not continue in use without repair within 90 days (or sooner depending on risk).
classification_of_findings.C3_improvement_recommended: No immediate danger but improvement advised for full compliance or to reduce risk. Note on report — no specific time requirement.
classification_of_findings.FI_further_investigation_required: Could not determine compliance — further investigation needed before declaration of compliance or non-compliance.

overall_verdict.satisfactory: No C1 or C2 observations — installation complies with applicable standard at the date of inspection
overall_verdict.unsatisfactory: One or more C1 or C2 observations — installation does not comply. Remedial work required.
